complete_workflow_guide.overview: Complete workflow guide for DevOps Concert Shell V2 mode covering both false positive detection and true positive package remediation flows. This ensures Bob handles both scenarios correctly in GitHub Actions automation.

start.action: Fetch GitHub issue with CVE details

actions.action: Query Concert API for CVE details and recommendations
Query NVD API for official CVE validation
Query GitHub Advisory or OSV as fallback if needed
Identify vulnerable functions and attack vectors

step.name: Analyze Source Code for False Positive Detection

actions.action: Trace data flow to vulnerable functions

steps.step: Create detailed analysis comment on GitHub issue
Include evidence: file paths, code snippets, reasoning
Do NOT create branch or PR
Do NOT output pr_number, pr_branch, or pr_url

outputs.output: No PR outputs (pr_number, pr_branch, pr_url remain empty)

steps.step: Update package manifest files (requirements.txt, package.json, etc.)
For Python: Run pip install --dry-run to verify
Create pull request with detailed description

when_to_classify_as_false_positive.criterion: Package is transitive dependency never directly used
Vulnerable functions are not called anywhere in codebase
Vulnerable code paths are unreachable due to application logic
Service is not exposed to untrusted input

step.name: Create GitHub Issue Comment
step.details: Use templates from 3_github_issue_comment_templates.xml Include: CVE context, code analysis, evidence, recommendation Tag DevOps engineer with @username

step.name: Do NOT Create Branch or PR
step.details: Skip all package remediation steps Do not create fix/cve-* branch Do not modify any files Do not commit or push Do not create pull request

step.name: Do NOT Output PR Metadata
step.details: Do not set pr_number output Do not set pr_branch output Do not set pr_url output Leave these outputs empty/undefined

step.name: Exit Workflow Successfully
step.details: Complete Bob Shell execution GitHub Actions will detect missing pr_number Workflow will skip PR-related steps Subsequent jobs will not run

github_issue_comment_requirements.requirement: Include CVE ID, package name, and severity
Include specific file paths and line numbers
Add code snippets showing non-vulnerable usage
Explain why it's a false positive

when_to_classify_as_true_positive.criterion: Vulnerable functions are directly called with user input
Service is exposed to untrusted users
Package is directly imported and used
When uncertain, default to TRUE POSITIVE (safer)

step.name: Create Remediation Branch
step.details: Branch naming: fix/cve-{CVE-ID}-{package-name} Example: fix/cve-2023-30861-flask Create from main/master branch Use git checkout -b or GitHub MCP

step.name: Update Package Manifests
step.details: Python: requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, Pipfile Node.js: package.json, package-lock.json Java: pom.xml, build.gradle Apply Concert's recommended version Update related dependencies if needed Add comment explaining CVE fix
step.example:
# Before flask==2.2.0 # Vulnerable to CVE-2023-30861
# After flask==2.3.3 # Fixed CVE-2023-30861 per Concert recommendation

step.name: Verify Package Compatibility
step.details: Python: pip install -r requirements.txt --dry-run Node.js: npm install --dry-run Check for transitive dependency conflicts Document any compatibility concerns

step.name: Commit Changes
Push Branch
Create Pull Request
step.details: Use GitHub MCP first, fallback to gh CLI Follow PR template from 6_pr_formatting_and_documentation.xml Include all required sections: - Security Vulnerability Remediation - Vulnerability Analysis - Changes Made - Remediation Strategy - Concert API Analysis - Impact Assessment - Validation Checklist - References

step.name: Link PR to Issue
Output PR Metadata

out_of_scope.item: Runtime code paths or business logic
Unit test fixes or compatibility changes

handoff_criteria.criterion: If package upgrade requires code changes → Hand off to vuln-remediation-concert mode
If tests fail after upgrade → Hand off to vuln-remediation-concert mode
If breaking changes detected → Document in PR, let CI/CD handle

concert_api_integration.title: Concert API Usage in Both Flows

research_phase.description: Both flows start with Concert API research
research_phase.purpose: Understand CVE context and get remediation recommendations

false_positive_update.description: False positive updates handled in same workflow after approval
false_positive_update.method: One PATCH request per CVE (simpler than batch update)

execution.step: Bob Shell is invoked again in devops-concert-shell-v2 mode
Bob reads the GitHub issue to extract CVE IDs and application name
Bob executes one PATCH request per CVE using the simplified endpoint
Bob posts success/failure comment on issue and closes it if successful

false_positive_update.example: curl -kX PATCH \ -H "Authorization: ${CONCERT_API_KEY}" \ -H "InstanceId: ${CONCERT_INSTANCE_ID}" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ "${CONCERT_HOSTNAME}/concert/ingestion/api/v1/vulnerability_assessments/cve/CVE-2023-32681/application/sample-python-app" \ -d '{"status": "False positive", "comment": "Application does not use vulnerable functions."}'

advantages.advantage: No need to query for findings_id or assessment_id
Direct CVE and application name mapping

false_positive_update.important: This endpoint IS called by Bob in Step 2A after DevOps approval. The workflow invokes Bob Shell again specifically to execute these Concert API updates.

true_positive_tracking.description: True positive remediation tracked via PR and issue
true_positive_tracking.tracking: PR linked to issue, Concert tracks via issue reference
true_positive_tracking.no_api_update: This mode does not update Concert status for true positives

github_actions_integration.title: How GitHub Actions Workflow Handles Both Flows

workflow_detection.step: Bob Shell executes in devops-concert-shell-v2 mode
If pr_number is empty → False positive detected
If pr_number exists → True positive, PR created

false_positive_workflow_behavior.step: Posts approval request comment on issue
Skips all PR-related steps (validation, comments, reviews)
Triggers Step 2A: False Positive Approval Gate
Subsequent jobs don't run (approval gate, tests, fixes)

true_positive_workflow_behavior.step: Adds approval request comment to PR
Subsequent jobs run (approval gate, tests, fixes)

best_practices.practice: Always perform false positive analysis BEFORE any package changes
When uncertain, default to TRUE POSITIVE (safer approach)
For false positives: Comment on issue, do NOT create PR
For true positives: Create PR with complete documentation
Never modify application code or tests in this mode
Provide specific evidence for false positive determinations
Follow PR template exactly for true positives
Consider transitive dependencies when upgrading packages

error.description: Cannot definitively determine if false positive
error.action: Default to TRUE POSITIVE and proceed with remediation
error.rationale: Better to upgrade unnecessarily than leave vulnerability

error.action: Document the failure and default to TRUE POSITIVE
error.rationale: Cannot perform analysis without code access

error.action: Proceed with external CVE research (NVD, GitHub Advisory)
error.rationale: Can still remediate using public CVE data

error.description: GitHub MCP fails for issue or PR operations
error.action: Fallback to gh CLI or authenticated REST API

summary.key_point: DevOps Concert Shell V2 mode handles TWO distinct workflows based on false positive analysis
FALSE POSITIVE: Comment on issue, tag engineer, exit without PR
TRUE POSITIVE: Create branch, update manifests, create PR with full documentation
GitHub Actions workflow detects which path was taken via pr_number output
When uncertain, always default to TRUE POSITIVE for safety
